intent_policy: This task is for benchmark evaluation ground truth only, not for writing generation.
The output must be a concise writing_spec object, not an author-facing writing intent.
Evaluate core scene function rather than exact scene replication.
Keep the combined natural-language content shorter than the source passage and never longer than the source passage.
Preserve central named entities, locations, important objects, concepts, organizations, or occasions only when their absence would make the scene identity fail.
Use compact required_narrative_units rather than a full synopsis or beat-by-beat outline.
Do not turn incidental geography, destinations, route labels, exact choreography, or replaceable physical mechanics into hard requirements unless they are central to the scene conflict.
For short action scenes, preserve behavior function and character pressure before exact physical mechanics.
Avoid exact dialogue, evidence spans, retrieval instructions, query plans, and generation instructions.
The output must be parseable JSON.
